message:
🌱 falco: Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless (#1949)

* 🌱 Add falco: Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless mission

@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "falco-2181-migrate-falco-image-from-debian-to-distroless",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "falco: Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless",
+    "description": "Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current falco deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your falco version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco\nhelm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working falco installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review falco configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant falco configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco\nkubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco\n```\n**Motivation**\n\nTo reduce attack surface and patching maintenance, we should use the most minimal base image for the Falco image as possible.\n\nCurrently debian:11-slim is used, however Google's distroless image would be more lightweight."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless",
+        "description": "Hey  , thanks for looking into this!\n\nSo, as you can see, there are two images for Falco one is the `no-driver` and the other is the self-contained Falco / `driver-loader` images. The two are very different and this issue is mostly related to the `no-driver` image _because it's so much easier to\n```yaml\ndocker run --rm -i -t \\\n           --privileged \\\n           -v /var/run/docker.sock:/host/var/run/docker.sock \\\n           -v /proc:/host/proc:ro \\\n           falcosecurity/falco-no-driver:latest falco --modern-bpf\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco\nkubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hey  , thanks for looking into this!\n\nSo, as you can see, there are two images for Falco one is the `no-driver` and the other is the self-contained Falco / `driver-loader` images. The two are very different and this issue is mostly related to the `no-driver` image _because it's so much easier to handle_ as you correctly identified.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "docker run --rm -i -t \\\n           --privileged \\\n           -v /var/run/docker.sock:/host/var/run/docker.sock \\\n           -v /proc:/host/proc:ro \\\n           falcosecurity/falco-no-driver:latest falco --modern-bpf"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "falco",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "falco"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco/issues/2181",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco"
+    },
+    "reactions": 7,
+    "comments": 26,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-03T06:33:03.063Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+      },

+        "title": "Apply the fix for Migrate Falco image from debian to distroless",
+        "description": "**What type of PR is this?**\n\n**Any specific area of the project related to this PR?**\n\n**What this PR does / why we need it**:\n\nAdds distroless dockerfiles for Falco. These should be smaller and less likely to include vulnerabilities when scanned (due to simply having less packages in them).\n\nNote there are two Dockerfiles, one of which builds from source, the other uses precompiled binaries.\n\n**Which issue(s) this PR fixes**:\r\n\n**Special notes for your reviewer**:\n\nThis a draft currently,\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",

+    ],

+      "summary": "**What type of PR is this?**\n\n**Any specific area of the project related to this PR?**\n\n**What this PR does / why we need it**:\n\nAdds distroless dockerfiles for Falco. These should be smaller and less likely to include vulnerabilities when scanned (due to simply having less packages in them).\n\nNote there are two Dockerfiles, one of which builds from source, the other uses precompiled binaries.",
